name: NTR Bharosa Pension Scheme
eligibility: Senior citizens, widows, persons with disabilities, and other eligible vulnerable residents of Andhra Pradesh can apply if they meet the scheme rules.
benefits: Monthly pension is provided through direct benefit transfer. The pension amount and category-specific rules are administered by the state pension system.
how_to_apply: Apply through the village or ward secretariat or the official pension portal with Aadhaar, bank account details, and required category proof.

name: YSR Cheyutha
eligibility: Women from eligible backward, scheduled caste, scheduled tribe, or minority households in Andhra Pradesh can apply if they satisfy the age and income rules.
benefits: Annual financial assistance is provided to support livelihood and self-employment. The payment is credited in installments as notified by the government.
how_to_apply: Apply through the village or ward secretariat with Aadhaar, bank account, income proof, and community certificate if required.

name: Amma Vodi
eligibility: Eligible mothers or guardians in Andhra Pradesh with school-going children under the notified school criteria can apply.
benefits: Annual financial assistance is given to support children's education and school attendance. The money is transferred directly to the beneficiary bank account.
how_to_apply: Apply through the school and village or ward secretariat using Aadhaar, bank account details, and child school enrollment records.

name: Jagananna Vidya Kanuka
eligibility: Students enrolled in government schools in Andhra Pradesh are eligible under the scheme rules.
benefits: Free school kits are supplied, including books, notebooks, uniform material, shoes, socks, and a belt. The kit is distributed through the school each academic year.
how_to_apply: No separate application is usually required; eligible students receive the kit through their government school. Schools verify enrollment and issue the materials.

name: Nadu-Nedu School Infrastructure Program
eligibility: Government school institutions in Andhra Pradesh covered under the state school improvement plan are eligible.
benefits: Schools receive infrastructure upgrades such as classrooms, toilets, drinking water, electrification, and other basic facilities. The work is implemented by the government for public schools.
how_to_apply: Implementation is department-driven; school heads and education officials coordinate through the school education department and local government channels.

name: YSR Rythu Bharosa
eligibility: Resident farmers in Andhra Pradesh with eligible cultivable land records and other notified farmer conditions can apply.
benefits: Financial support is provided to eligible farmers as annual investment assistance. The state benefit is paid along with the linked central PM-Kisan support where applicable.
how_to_apply: Apply through the agriculture department or village secretariat with Aadhaar, bank account details, and land records.

name: Free Electricity to Farmers
eligibility: Eligible agricultural pump-set consumers in Andhra Pradesh with registered connections and valid farmer records can receive support.
benefits: Electricity charges for eligible agricultural connections are borne by the government as per scheme rules. Support is extended through the power utilities and state reimbursement mechanism.
how_to_apply: Apply or get verified through the electricity department and local revenue or agriculture offices with land and consumer details.

name: YSR Bima
eligibility: Unorganised workers and notified vulnerable families in Andhra Pradesh are eligible if they meet the scheme conditions.
benefits: Financial relief is provided to the family in case of death or disability of the insured or eligible worker. The assistance amount depends on the category and notified rules.
how_to_apply: Apply through the village or ward secretariat or the labour department with Aadhaar, bank account details, and proof of occupation or registration.

name: Pedalandariki Illu
eligibility: Homeless or eligible low-income families in Andhra Pradesh meeting the housing criteria can apply.
benefits: House sites and housing assistance are provided under the state housing program. The benefit is aimed at construction of permanent homes for eligible families.
how_to_apply: Apply through the village or ward secretariat, revenue office, or housing department with Aadhaar, income proof, and family details.

name: Jagananna Colonies
eligibility: Beneficiary families selected under the state housing and house-site allotment rules are eligible.
benefits: Planned layouts and residential plots are developed for eligible families. Basic civic infrastructure is provided in the colony development process.
how_to_apply: Selection is done through local revenue and housing officials using the state housing beneficiary database and supporting documents.

name: YSR Arogya Asara
eligibility: Patients discharged after eligible government hospital treatment in Andhra Pradesh can receive support if they meet scheme conditions.
benefits: Financial assistance is provided during recovery after treatment in approved public hospitals. The support is credited directly to the eligible beneficiary.
how_to_apply: The hospital and health department process the claim using Aadhaar, discharge records, and bank account details.

name: YSR Kanti Velugu
eligibility: Residents of Andhra Pradesh are eligible for eye screening and related services under the state program.
benefits: Free eye screening, spectacles, and referral support are provided where medically required. Cataract and other eye-care services are arranged through government health facilities.
how_to_apply: Visit the designated screening camp, public health center, or hospital as announced by the health department.

name: YSR Sampoorna Poshana
eligibility: Pregnant women, lactating mothers, and eligible children covered under the state nutrition program in Andhra Pradesh can receive support.
benefits: Nutrition support, supplementary food, and maternal-child health assistance are provided through government channels. Services are delivered regularly through anganwadi and health systems.
how_to_apply: Register through the anganwadi center, village secretariat, or ICDS office with Aadhaar and maternal or child health records.

name: NTR Vidyonnathi
eligibility: Eligible students from scheduled caste and other notified communities in Andhra Pradesh who are preparing for civil services can apply.
benefits: Free coaching support is provided through empaneled coaching institutions. Selected students receive training and related academic support.
how_to_apply: Apply through the social welfare department or notified online portal with community certificate, education records, and Aadhaar.

name: Free Electricity to SC Households
eligibility: Scheduled caste households in Andhra Pradesh with low monthly electricity consumption and valid service connections are eligible.
benefits: Electricity charges are paid by the government for eligible households under the notified consumption limit. The support is settled directly with the distribution company.
how_to_apply: Apply through the social welfare department or local secretariat with Aadhaar, electricity consumer details, and caste proof.

name: Jagananna Vidya Deevena
eligibility: Eligible students from BC, EBC, Kapu and other notified categories in Andhra Pradesh studying post-matric courses can apply if they meet the income and attendance rules.
benefits: Full fee reimbursement is provided for eligible students. The amount is paid in quarterly installments to the mother or guardian account as per scheme rules.
how_to_apply: Apply through the JnanaBhumi or department portal and the village or ward secretariat with Aadhaar, bank account details, income certificate, caste certificate if applicable, and college records.

name: Jagananna Vasathi Deevena
eligibility: Eligible BC, EBC and Kapu students in Andhra Pradesh pursuing ITI, polytechnic, degree or higher post-matric courses can apply if they satisfy the income and attendance norms.
benefits: Financial support is provided for food and hostel expenses. The amount is paid in installments to the mother or guardian account based on course type.
how_to_apply: Apply through the JnanaBhumi or department portal and local secretariat with Aadhaar, bank account details, income certificate, caste certificate if applicable, and college admission proof.

name: Maintenance of BC Hostels
eligibility: BC students from eligible income groups in Andhra Pradesh can seek admission in government BC hostels subject to seat availability and hostel rules.
benefits: Free accommodation, meals, books, bedding material, uniforms, and study support are provided. Hostel services are maintained by the BC Welfare Department.
how_to_apply: Apply at the concerned BC hostel or district BC welfare office with Aadhaar, caste certificate, school or college admission proof, and income certificate if required.

name: Pre-metric Scholarships for BC Students
eligibility: BC students studying in Classes 9 and 10 in eligible government or aided schools in Andhra Pradesh can apply if the family income is within the notified limit.
benefits: Scholarship support is provided to reduce school education costs. The assistance is released according to the state scholarship rules.
how_to_apply: Apply through the school and the JnanaBhumi or department system with Aadhaar, bank account details, income certificate, and caste certificate.

name: Post-metric Scholarships for BC Students
eligibility: BC students in Andhra Pradesh studying intermediate and above courses can apply if they meet the income and course eligibility conditions.
benefits: Scholarship support is provided for post-matric studies. The benefit is sanctioned as per the applicable fee reimbursement and maintenance rules.
how_to_apply: Apply through the JnanaBhumi or department portal with Aadhaar, bank account details, income certificate, caste certificate, and college admission records.

name: Incentives for Inter-caste Couples
eligibility: Inter-caste married couples in Andhra Pradesh who meet the notified community and marriage conditions can apply.
benefits: A cash incentive is provided to encourage social integration through inter-caste marriage. The amount is sanctioned as per the current government order.
how_to_apply: Apply through the district social welfare or BC welfare office with marriage certificate, Aadhaar, caste certificates, and bank account details.

name: Training to BC Law Graduates
eligibility: BC law graduates in Andhra Pradesh can apply if they are selected under the training programme and meet the notified conditions.
benefits: Selected graduates receive training under senior government advocates or public prosecutors. A monthly stipend and support for books and related expenses are provided.
how_to_apply: Apply through the BC welfare department or the notified portal with degree certificate, Aadhaar, caste certificate, and bank account details.

name: NTR Videshi Vidyadharana Overseas Scholarships
eligibility: Eligible BC students from Andhra Pradesh pursuing higher studies abroad can apply if they meet the notified academic and family income limits.
benefits: Scholarship assistance is provided for overseas higher education. The support amount is released in installments as per the scheme guidelines.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official scholarship portal with admission letter, Aadhaar, income certificate, caste certificate, passport and bank account details.

name: BC Cooperative Societies Registration
eligibility: Eligible BC community groups and cooperative society members in Andhra Pradesh can apply according to the community-specific rules.
benefits: Registered cooperative societies can access government-recognized organizational support and related welfare benefits. The scheme supports community-based economic activity.
how_to_apply: Apply through the district BC welfare office with society documents, community proof, Aadhaar, and other registration records.

name: Mahatma Jyotiba Phule Andhra Pradesh Backward Classes Welfare Residential Educational Institutions Society
eligibility: BC students in Andhra Pradesh who are admitted into the notified residential educational institutions can benefit based on merit and admission rules.
benefits: Residential education, accommodation, meals, and academic support are provided in BC welfare institutions. Schools and hostels are maintained by the state.
how_to_apply: Apply through the admission process of the concerned residential school or college with school records, Aadhaar, caste certificate, and income proof if required.

name: Jagananna Chedodu
eligibility: Eligible tailors, barbers, washersmen, and traditional occupational groups in Andhra Pradesh can apply if they meet the income and occupation rules.
benefits: Annual financial assistance is provided to support traditional livelihoods. The aid is transferred directly to the beneficiary account.
how_to_apply: Apply through the village or ward secretariat with Aadhaar, bank account details, occupation proof, and income certificate.

name: YSR Kalyanamasthu
eligibility: Eligible families in Andhra Pradesh who arrange marriages for girls from notified categories under the scheme rules can apply.
benefits: Marriage assistance is provided to support the marriage of eligible girls. The amount is sanctioned directly to the beneficiary family.
how_to_apply: Apply through the village or ward secretariat with marriage proof, Aadhaar, bank account details, and caste or income documents if required.

name: YSR Shaadi Tohfa
eligibility: Eligible minority brides in Andhra Pradesh who meet the notified marriage and income conditions can apply.
benefits: Marriage assistance is provided to support eligible minority women at the time of marriage. The benefit is transferred as per state rules.
how_to_apply: Apply through the minority welfare department or village and ward secretariat with Aadhaar, marriage certificate, bank account details, and income proof.

name: YSR Law Nestham
eligibility: Young advocates enrolled in Andhra Pradesh who meet the age, enrollment, and practice conditions can apply.
benefits: Monthly financial assistance is provided to support new advocates in the early years of practice. The support is released directly to the eligible advocate.
how_to_apply: Apply through the law department or notified online portal with enrollment certificate, Aadhaar, bank account details, and practice documents.

name: YSR Matsyakara Bharosa
eligibility: Fishermen families in Andhra Pradesh who depend on fishing and meet the notified residence and livelihood conditions can apply.
benefits: Financial support is provided to fisherfolk, including assistance during the fishing ban period when notified. The benefit is paid directly to eligible beneficiaries.
how_to_apply: Apply through the fisheries department or village secretariat with Aadhaar, bank account details, fishing identity or livelihood proof, and family documents.
